category_title: Able speaker who monopolizes and struggles with turn-taking
student_description: John is a highly verbal student who monopolizes conversations, frequently interrupts, and struggles to listen to peers in unstructured settings. The evidence reveals he uniquely needs physical and visual structuring of ideas—such as writing notes and placing them next to photos—to regulate his impulses. He also requires high-interest hooks, specifically humor, to motivate him to value and listen to peer contributions.

criteria.rationale: John struggles with unstructured verbal turn-taking but can regulate his impulses when ideas are physically written down and paired with visual aids.
criteria.check: The tutor's response must involve writing down ideas (John's or his peers') and physically or visually linking them to photos, props, or notes to structure the conversation.

criteria.rationale: John tends to ignore his peers unless their contributions capture his attention through high-interest elements like humor.
criteria.check: The tutor must encourage peers to make funny suggestions or frame peer ideas in a humorous way to motivate John to listen.
